predictions.rationale: Market reads Mexico as heavy favourites (68%) and intel agrees: El Tri are unbeaten in their 2026 friendlies, play a sold-out Azteca at ~2,250m altitude, and own a strong World Cup home record. South Africa arrive in weak form (no win in five) and are missing wingers Morena and Nkota. Multiple tipsters lean Mexico clean sheet, supporting 2-0.
predictions.disagreement: Some tipsters question Mexico's attacking depth beyond Raul Jimenez and favour a tighter 1-0, but still side with Mexico.

sources.title: ESPN: Mexico vs South Africa team news
Sky Sports Group A guide

predictions.rationale: Market sees a near coin-flip: South Korea nominally ahead (36%) but Czechia level (33%) and a high draw probability (30%). Korea's low-scoring profile and Czech long-haul travel into altitude both cap goals, while Czechia carry playoff grit and Schick's aerial threat. Son gives Korea the quality edge, but the stalemate is the value lean.
predictions.disagreement: Bookmaker price has Korea marginally favoured; tipster consensus instead calls the 1-1 draw the value play.

sources.title: Goal.com: South Korea vs Czech Republic preview
Squawka: South Korea vs Czech Republic predictions

predictions.rationale: Market favours Canada (53%) at a sold-out BMO Field, and the key absence is now confirmed: Alphonso Davies has returned to team training but coach Jesse Marsch stated unequivocally (June 8) that the left back will NOT play the opener, targeting a later group game. Davies is Canada's best creator and pressing engine; his absence thins an already-stretched squad. Jonathan David leads the line and the narrow home edge holds, but the slim market margin barely covers the Davies-shaped hole against a Bosnia side carrying Demirovic, Tabakovic and Dzeko.
predictions.disagreement: Without Davies several tipsters have shifted to the draw as a live play; the 53% market may not have fully priced the depth of his impact.

sources.title: Goal: Davies admits injuries 'mentally draining', provides fitness update
Yahoo Sports: Why Davies missing the opener is a huge blow to Canada

predictions.rationale: Market makes the USA a modest home favourite (48%) at SoFi, and tipsters align on a low-scoring USA win — Paraguay conceded only 10 in 18 CONMEBOL qualifiers under Alfaro. The live unpriced risk is first-choice CB Chris Richards (ankle ligament injury, still a doubt), which weakens the back line against a disciplined Albirroja. USA won the November friendly 2-1.
predictions.disagreement: Paraguay's elite defensive record makes the draw a reasonable contrarian play; if Richards is ruled out the USA edge narrows further.

sources.title: Goal.com: USA vs Paraguay preview
ESPN: Richards out of final tuneup

predictions.rationale: Market is emphatic at 78% for Switzerland and the intel agrees with no counter-signal. The Swiss went unbeaten in qualifying with four clean sheets and carry a healthy experienced core (Xhaka, Embolo, Akanji) with no fresh injuries, while Qatar lost five qualifiers and a warm-up to Ireland. Nothing unpriced points toward an upset.

sources.title: ESPN: Xhaka to captain Switzerland, squad revealed
Footy Accumulators: Qatar vs Switzerland tips

predictions.rationale: Market backs Brazil at 58% but the injury picture remains severe: Neymar's June 8 MRI showed 'good progress within expected parameters', yet he is still not anticipated in the matchday squad for the opener (return targeted for the Haiti game, June 20), and both Estêvão and Rodrygo are out — stripping three of Brazil's planned wide-attacking trio. Vinícius Jr. and Raphinha must carry the attack alone. Morocco arrive sharp (1-1 draw with Norway in final friendly, no absences). Brazil's residual quality gap justifies the pick, but confidence is capped firmly — this is materially closer than the 58% implies.
predictions.disagreement: Multiple previews now treat this as close to a 50/50 given Brazil's injuries; Morocco could win if Vinícius and Raphinha are contained.

sources.title: ESPN: Neymar (calf) may miss World Cup opener
Morocco World News: Morocco draws 1-1 with Norway before opener

predictions.rationale: Market favours Scotland at 63% and tipsters lean firmly the same way on the back of strong warm-ups (4-1 Curacao, 4-0 Bolivia) and a midfield edge through McTominay and McGinn versus debutants Haiti. Scotland did lose Billy Gilmour to injury and Hanley has a knee doubt, but their European-league depth still clearly outclasses the 83rd-ranked Haitians.

sources.title: ESPN: Scotland World Cup squad announced
Footy Accumulators: Haiti vs Scotland tips

predictions.rationale: Market and tipsters both favour Turkey (56%) on the strength of Guler, Yildiz, Calhanoglu and a strong recent run including a draw with Spain. An unpriced negative for Australia reinforces it: Riley McGree is out for the whole tournament (hamstring) and Irvine is only just back from a long layoff, thinning the Socceroos' midfield. Australia's set-piece threat keeps confidence moderate.
predictions.disagreement: Some tipsters flag Australia at long odds as the best value despite picking Turkey, viewing the favourite as slightly short.

sources.title: Dimers: Australia vs Turkey prediction
Yahoo Sports: World Cup injuries/squads tracker

predictions.rationale: Market is emphatic at 90% and the intel fully backs it: Germany ride a nine-match winning run (4-0 Finland, 2-1 USA) versus World Cup debutants Curacao, ranked 82nd. The only wrinkle, Neuer's calf, is resolved with Nagelsmann confirming the keeper starts; nothing argues against a comfortable multi-goal home win.

sources.title: Germany vs Curacao preview & team news
Sky Sports Group E guide

predictions.rationale: Market makes the Netherlands a clear favourite at 47% with the draw and Japan level at ~26%, and the key unpriced intel strengthens the Dutch: Japan lost talisman winger Kaoru Mitoma to a hamstring injury (confirmed omission) having earlier lost Minamino to an ACL tear, gutting their attacking threat. Backing the home favourite is justified, though Japan's organisation keeps the draw live.

sources.title: ESPN: Mitoma ruled out of World Cup
Goal.com: Netherlands vs Japan preview

predictions.rationale: Market is genuinely split: Ecuador nominal favourite (39%) but the draw close behind (34%), reflecting a cagey opener neither side can afford to lose. Ecuador kept the best CONMEBOL qualifying defence (5 goals in 18) and have Caicedo available after an amnesty overturned his ban. With both defences organised and away/draw nearly tied, the draw offers the best value over the slim Ecuador edge.
predictions.disagreement: Market's single most likely outcome is an Ecuador win (39%); tipsters and the cagey-opener profile lean draw/under-2.5, so I take the draw.

sources.title: Opta Analyst: World Cup 2026 Group E predictions
FourFourTwo: Caicedo eligibility for opener

predictions.rationale: Market favours Sweden at 50% on the strength of the Gyokeres-Isak forward line, the clearest source of quality in this tie. The caveat is real: Isak endured an injury-hit season and Tunisia, under new coach Lamouchi, conceded zero goals across WC qualifying and are well-drilled defensively, pointing to a tight match the home side edges via a single moment.
predictions.disagreement: Some previews frame this as Tunisia's best chance to take points given their clean-sheet record, nudging toward the draw, but Sweden's superior attack keeps the home pick.

sources.title: OneFootball: Sweden name Isak and Gyokeres
FIFA: Lamouchi names Tunisia squad

predictions.rationale: Market is emphatic at 88% for the reigning Euro champions and No.2-ranked side, and the intel backs a comfortable win: Rodri is fit and Lamine Yamal — rested for the Iraq warm-up as a precaution — is expected to be available for the opener in Atlanta. Cape Verde are World Cup debutants and one of the smallest nations ever to qualify; Spain's loss of Carvajal (toe) and Fermín (foot) barely dents their depth. A fast start and clean sheet are the script.
predictions.disagreement: None of substance — a clear class gap; the only question is the margin, with debutants capable of frustrating early.

sources.title: ESPN: Spain coach expects Yamal fit for opener vs Cape Verde
RotoWire: World Cup Group H preview

predictions.rationale: Market backs Belgium at 57% as the group's top seed, and form supports it — Rudi Garcia's side hammered 10-man Tunisia 5-0 in their final warm-up with De Bruyne, Doku and Openda driving a fluid attack. Egypt are the live threat: Hossam Hassan's pragmatic, hard-to-break block plus Salah's world-class counter (involved in 60% of Egypt's qualifying goals) and Marmoush keep the draw and upset in play. Belgium's quality edge takes it, but Salah caps confidence.
predictions.disagreement: Egypt's defensive discipline and Salah's matchwinning ceiling make them a popular contrarian draw pick; Opta gives them a 20% chance of topping the group.

sources.title: Ahram Online: Belgium warm up with 5-0 win over 10-man Tunisia
Opta Analyst: World Cup 2026 Group G predictions

predictions.rationale: Market is clear at 66% for Uruguay and the intel agrees with no counter-signal: La Celeste field a European-and-elite-South-American core (Núñez, Valverde, plus Bentancur and the Real Madrid contingent) against a Saudi side built almost entirely on the domestic league. Saudi Arabia will sit deep and try to break, so the books expect a controlled, low-scoring Uruguay win rather than a rout.
predictions.disagreement: Saudi Arabia's deep block makes a frustrating 1-0 or even a draw the contrarian angle if Al Dawsari nicks a moment, but quality points firmly to Uruguay.

sources.title: Dimers: Uruguay vs Saudi Arabia updated prediction
MLSsoccer: World Cup Group H preview

predictions.rationale: Market makes Iran a slight favourite (50%) and form supports it — Iran won three of their last five (2-0 Mali, 5-0 Costa Rica, 3-1 Gambia), scoring 10 and conceding three, with Taremi leading the line and a solid defensive base. New Zealand arrive cold (one win in five) and lean heavily on Chris Wood for goals. Expect a tight, controlled opener Iran edge through quality and game management.
predictions.disagreement: Some tipsters see value in the All Whites' physicality and set-piece threat at long odds, nudging the draw, but Iran's defensive base and recent scoring run hold the pick.

sources.title: Dimers: Iran vs New Zealand updated prediction
Goal.com: Iran vs New Zealand preview
